programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly

programme.title: Walker, Texas Ranger
programme.desc: Walker implements a gruelling six-week boot camp programme in an attempt to rehabilitate a group of hardened criminals. Chuck Norris stars

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Agent Avery Ryan and her team investigate when car service company ZoGo's dispatch software is hacked, leading to the death of a private military contractor, who is electrocuted by a driver posing as a ZoGo chauffeur. Investigations suggest both a phishing scammer and a disgruntled former employee may be the culprit. Meanwhile, Daniel confronts a tragic event from his past. Patricia Arquette stars

programme.title: NCIS: Los Angeles
programme.desc: The agents investigate the deaths of two men killed in a shoot-out, but have to rely on a former naval intelligence officer suffering from post-traumatic stress disorder to help them close the case

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A US marine visits the home of his former captain, only to find the man has been killed, and the killer is waiting inside the property. While the soldier escapes with his life, the attacker gets away too. The subsequent chain of evidence leads Gibbs and Bishop to Afghanistan

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs and the team capture computer hacker Ajay Khan, who was recently responsible for hacking into NCIS's supposedly ultra-secure multiple-threat alert centre. They believe he could hold the key to tracking down the most-wanted cyber terrorist in the world, who may be plotting an attack using a bomb containing a strain of the Ebola virus. When Khan refuses to co-operate, Ziva and DiNozzo take him to Guantanamo Bay to be placed with people he had previously betrayed. Guest starring Vik Sahay (Chuck)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the case of a marine who dropped dead while scaling a wall on an assault course, and a fellow soldier reveals that he was heavily involved with an underground fight club, where competitors would battle for cash. Meanwhile, having returned to work after compassionate leave, Vance discovers paperwork and a key in his late wife's effects that lead him to a safety deposit box containing a stash of cash

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: While transporting the corpse of Lt Gordon Roth to the morgue, a flat tyre brings Ducky and Jimmy to a halt and they are abducted and taken to a remote cabin - where one of their captors wants the post-mortem to take place. Back at the scene, Abby finds a blood sample that does not belong to the victim, who in fact bore little resemblance to the picture of Roth in his old school yearbook. Guest starring Valerie Cruz (The Following)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A woman is stabbed to death after stepping out of her shower, becoming the latest victim of the Dead Rose Slasher. Gibbs is approached by his old friend and barber Frankie Dean, who shares his concern that his son Cameron may be the killer. He explains that a photo-fit released to the media resembles him, and the attacks stopped during a period coinciding with his three-year stint in the navy. Meanwhile, Tony has to take probationary agent Ned Dorneget under his wing

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: US Marine sergeant Teddy Lemere is killed by a sniper in Afghanistan just after his sniffer dog helps guide a young child out of a minefield. Back in the US, his widow Ruby tells Gibbs and the team that she had recently received a message from her husband saying he might have upset a few people. Abby retrieves a tiny shard of the sniper's bullet from Ted's helmet and finds that it was made in America

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A marine lieutenant's body falls from the sky and the team links the man's death to the disappearance of a Navy chaplain thought to have been kidnapped by rebels working with Colombian crime cartels. Ziva and Tony are dispatched to conduct a rescue operation and enlist the help of former Interpol agent Monique Lisson, while Gibbs discovers there was more to the chaplain's outreach mission than meets the eye. Crime drama, guest starring Karina Lombard (The L Word) and Jamie Lee Curtis (A Fish Called Wanda)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A fire crew discovers a man's body after putting out a raging blaze at a warehouse belonging to a Navy-contracted electronics company, and Gibbs recognises the victim as Carter Plimpton - a former member of an NCIS black-ops unit. Meanwhile, a member of the Baltimore police department helping with the case appears to have a grudge against Tony

programme.title: CSI: Cyber
programme.desc: Crime drama spin-off, starring Patricia Arquette as Special Agent Avery Ryan, head of the FBI's cyber crime division. Also starring James Van Der Beek

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A man's mistress is found murdered, adding a further unpleasant dimension to his already acrimonious divorce - and before the CSIs can begin their investigation, another body turns up. A suspect is eventually identified, and the case goes to court, but a wily defence attorney manages to have Delko, whose evidence is vital, ruled unfit to testify

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates an arson attack that looks like a case of revenge on an ex-lover and her new partner, leading him to meet Yelina. However, as events proceed, he finds himself with no option but to sack a team member whose personal problems are starting to disrupt the inquiry. Drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A suspected carjacker is murdered and the team believes he may have been involved in drug smuggling. To complicate matters, evidence suggests that Ryan, who is now working for local TV, could have inadvertently led the killer to his victim, a possibility that further distances him from his former colleagues. Crime drama, starring David Caruso

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: A serial killer runs amok, marking all his victims with a Y-shaped incision on their chests, and the CSIs risk their lives to bring him to justice. However, while the first suspect is in custody, another victim is found with the same signature injury. Crime drama, starring David Caruso, Emily Procter and Adam Rodriguez

programme.title: CSI: Miami
programme.desc: Horatio investigates the murder of a probation officer and is shocked to discover that DNA evidence suggests the killer is connected to him. Yelina takes up the case, and it emerges drugs are at the heart of the crime. Meanwhile, Eric is upset at Calleigh's new relationship with Jake Berkeley. Drama, starring David Caruso and Sofia Milos

Elsewhere, the British airmen head to the canal to rendezvous with the submarine waiting for them. Comedy, starring Gorden Kaye, Hilary Minster and Kim Hartman

programme.title: Last of the Summer Wine

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuth must stop a much-loved institution from going off the rails when the local stationmaster is murdered. Mark Williams stars

programme.title: Maisie Raine
programme.desc: The stalking of a TV game show host seems an ideal case for trainee detective Kyprianou to cut his teeth on, but Maisie soon regrets her decision to hand him the investigation. Police drama, starring Pauline Quirke and Steve John Shepherd

programme.title: New Tricks
programme.desc: The ex-coppers find themselves under the microscope when a psychologist arrives to conduct a study into older men in the workplace - but she soon reveals a more personal reason for her visit. It turns out that five years ago her brother, a cycle courier, was the victim of a hit-and-run that left him unable to recall anything prior to the incident. Now he says he was targeted for the parcel he was carrying, which was stolen at the scene - or so he believes. Sally Phillips guest stars

programme.title: Spooks
programme.desc: MI5 are called in when a family-owned bank is robbed to the tune of $1billion. As the team races to find the missing money and the culprit, undercover agent Danny discovers the firm has many dark secrets, and that someone in the highest echelons of the British Government is making sure they do not get out. Guest starring Robert Hardy, Oliver Ford Davies, Julie Cox and Sophie Okonedo

programme.desc: Feature-length episode. Nikki investigates the discovery of human bones on a former traveller site which could be the remains of a senior police officer's missing teenage daughter. Emilia Fox and Tom Ward star

Tim McInnerny guest stars alongside David Tennant and Catherine Tate

programme.title: Doctor Who
programme.desc: Part one of two. Former sidekick Martha Jones summons the Time Lord back to modern-day Earth, where an old enemy lies in wait. With mysterious devices spreading across the world, Donna discovers even her own family is not safe from the alien threat. Freema Agyeman and Christopher Ryan guest star

programme.title: Inspector George Gently
programme.desc: After an unidentified burnt body is found near an RAF base, Gently works alongside Special Branch officers who suspect the victim was involved with the IRA. However, the discovery that a large cache of guns has gone missing turns the case into a hostage situation. Feature-length episode starring Martin Shaw as the 1960s Northumberland detective, with Lee Ingleby and Robert Glenister

programme.title: The Brokenwood Mysteries

Suspicion falls on the neighbour and his new wife, who was known to have had ongoing problems with Philip over his unpaid bar tab at her pub. However, as Mike and the team dig further into the mystery more suspects come into play. Neill Rea stars

programme.desc: A woman is murdered and the forensic evidence takes the team to a cosmetic clinic where the victim had been receiving various treatments seemingly for free, but Nikki and Silva are frustrated by the CEO of Hamilton Ashe hospital when he blocks access to Karen's medical records. programme.title: The Bill

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel, starring Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha. A wealthy eligible bachelor rents a property in Hertfordshire, causing a flurry of anticipation among the ladies of the district. However, it transpires he also brought along a friend - the haughty and aloof Fitzwilliam Darcy

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Mrs Bennet spots an opportunity to marry off another of her daughters with the arrival of clergyman Mr Collins. However, Elizabeth is unable to take his proposal seriously, being too preoccupied with a handsome newcomer who poisons her mind further against Mr Darcy. Alison Steadman, Jennifer Ehle and David Bamber star in Andrew Davies' adaptation of Jane Austen's classic novel

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Elizabeth visits Mr Collins once Charlotte has accepted his proposal of marriage and confides in Jane that she regards him as one of the stupidest men in England. Mrs Bennet sizes up George Wickham as a potential suitor for her daughter - but he has set his heart on society heiress Miss King

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Rejected by Elizabeth, Darcy decides to return to Rosling Park - and resolves to reveal the truth about Wickham's character in a letter. Colin Firth, Jennifer Ehle, Alison Steadman, Benjamin Whitrow and Julia Sawalha star

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Despite Miss Bingley's efforts to keep them apart, Darcy and Elizabeth's relationship grows more affectionate - until bad news arrives from Longbourn about Lydia and Wickham. Jane Austen adaptation, starring Jennifer Ehle, Colin Firth and Julia Sawalha

programme.title: Pride and Prejudice
programme.desc: Wickham's elopement with Lydia makes Elizabeth see him for what he really is, while Bingley proposes to Jane - an offer she readily accepts. Colin Firth and Jennifer Ehle star

programme.desc: Mayor Makepeace hires Hetty to track down his runaway daughter, revealing that she has absconded with her boyfriend and could be in grave danger since both suffer from learning disabilities. Drama, starring Patricia Routledge and Derek Benfield

programme.desc: Sister Julienne helps a woman from Pakistan who is unable to have children, but who has found to her shock that her husband is bringing a second wife into their home, who is already eight months pregnant. programme.title: Rebus
programme.desc: The wife of prominent charity campaigner Gregor Jack is murdered, and Rebus wonders whether her husband committed the crime - especially as he was recently spotted patronising a brothel. Ken Stott stars

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in North Yorkshire look for county lines drug dealers in Harrogate, while at the auction one fraudster is about to have their home sold to pay back the money they made illegally. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates a Surrey property overwhelmed with rubbish and talks to the owner about their struggles with hoarding. In west London, probate genealogist Richard tracks down a deceased homeowner's relatives to help them claim their inheritance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. Fed up with the Land Army, Joyce forces Tucker into an admission of guilt, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana is part of Jack's plan to escape with Bea and the baby. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Liam Garrigan and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. The duo meet Ivan Day, who has one of the most extensive collections of recipe books in the UK, and share stories of renowned food writers such as Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Riyadh Khalaf host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and the Rich Brothers compete to design an Ibiza-themed party garden for a family in Wokingham, Berkshire. All concerned hope to come up with some cool ideas to transform this unloved plot into a laid-back Balearic chill out lounge on a budget of £3,000

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A hypnotist is booked to provide the entertainment at the triplets' baptism - and Agnes soon finds herself under his spell. Meanwhile, the matriarch's kitchen is falling apart - but that turns out to be the least of her problems when son Mark delivers some heartbreaking news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Adventure, recounted through Tonto's memories of the earliest efforts from himself and the Lone Ranger to subdue local villainy and bring justice to the American Old West. Johnny Depp, Armie Hammer, Tom Wilkinson and Helena Bonham Carter star

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Shivi Ramoutar, along with special guest Cat Deeley. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the second episode, Nadiya shares recipes for her most indulgent desserts, from frozen blueberry and banana cheesecake to husband's favourite jam roly poly. Later, Nadiya also shares her tips and tricks for her picture perfect tutti frutti pavlova

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury Antiques and Collectors Fair in Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. The host also pops across to Bristol to meet the maker of cigar box guitars

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v St Helens (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at AJ Bell Stadium. Saints will be looking for revenge after Warrington denied them a league and cup double with a shock 18-4 victory in last year's final. With analysis from Jamie Jones-Buchanan and Jon Wilkin and commentary by Dave Woods and Matt Newsum

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited young inventor, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly, beginning with Movies. Hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, each special will see the judges - Craig Revel Horwood, Motsi Mabuse, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli - as well all the Strictly professional dancers and special guests, reminisce about the iconic dances, the high-octane group numbers and the standout moments from the movie-themed weeks over the years

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, the contestants are Patsy Kensit, Jake Canuso, Jenny Hanley, David Hamilton, Chris Jarvis, Pui Fan Lee, Andrea McLean and Linda Robson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents, as regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by one-time Bake Off champion Nadiya Hussain, comic and actor Michael Smiley, and comedians Bob Mortimer and Diane Morgan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed the liar of the week

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. The Gunners and Red Devls both had high hopes of strong campaigns after each ending their domestic campaigns on a high last time out, and will have expected home wins. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Cleveland Browns and Cincinnati Bengals. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world

programme.title: Papadopoulos & Sons (2012)
programme.desc: A Greek millionaire loses everything he has in a financial crisis, leaving him with only one asset - his share in the fish and chip shop his father used to run. He is forced to return to his roots and work with his laidback brother to revive the family business. Comedy, starring Stephen Dillane, Georgia Groome and Georges Corraface

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The Andrew Marr Show
programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics London
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate from the region

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mark and Gill Johnson fly out to Perth for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved there permanently. Aged in their forties, the pair know it's either now or never, but as an only child Mark has split loyalties. Nicki Chapman follows their progress in reaching a decision

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley marks Harvest in Saddleworth, on the Yorkshire-Lancashire border, and Katie Piper meets a Christian minister helping Welsh farmers through times of crisis

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander explore a detached house in Chesterton, Staffordshire, a former police office in Netherton in the Midlands and a Victorian house in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks for items to upcycle at Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland, where she comes across a broken dining table, three fabric chairs, and a collection of lawn bowls

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: With a big game looming against Manchester United. West Ham's leading scorer Martha Thomas is still suffering from an injury, and seeks emotional support from her girlfriend Ellie

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: The experts take on a surveyors' theodolite dating back 100 years, a brass bell from a Victorian ice-cream business, and a 60-year-old leather football. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: The Rich brothers and Charlie Dimmock compete for the chance to transform the Romsey garden of Harry and Anna, turning it from a boring blank patch into a sophisticated party space

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Dominic Chinea uses his expertise to make a dilapidated and unsafe go-kart roadworthy once again. Kirsten Ramsay faces an unusual challenge in the shape of a papier mache cat

programme.title: The Boss
programme.desc: Susan Calman hosts the quiz in which one contestant is put in control and has to use judgement and tactics to decide which of the other players is best suited to answer

programme.title: Make Me a Dealer
programme.desc: Paul Martin is in Croydon, south London, teaching actress Anne and government employee Paul a few tricks of the antiques-buying trade to see who can make the most profit

programme.title: A Place to Call Home
programme.desc: Matt's insistence on finding the mother who gave Georgie up for adoption has unforseen consequences for himself and Olivia. When top surgeon Neil Burton arrives at Inverness Hospital it becomes immediately obvious that he is not there for Henry's job. Anna has a shock for Mrs Collins

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes from the BBC archives

programme.title: Oceans
programme.desc: The first of two episodes exploring the Indian Ocean, which accounts for 13 per cent of the Earth's surface and covers 26 million square miles. The expedition visits a 'coral nursery', one of the few places in the world where an extraordinary technique is being used to repair damaged reefs. Paul Rose and the team also head to a small community in Mozambique where shark fishing is an established practice

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents from Norwich Cathedral, once home to Benedictine monks. Experts Thomas Plant and David Fletcher pick out some items to take to auction. Among them are an old astronomical map and a watercolour by a local artist. Paul also explores the history of the nearby Broads.

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Beekeeper Valentine and market gardener John meet their regular customers and discover new ones selling their skincare products and organic vegetables at St George's weekly market in central Belfast

programme.title: Celebrity Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: One-time Blue Peter presenters Valerie Singleton and Diane Louise Jordan go searching for the antiques and collectibles they hope will raise them money at auction in Stratford-upon-Avon, with expert help from Paul Laidlaw and Philip Serrell. Along the way, Valerie finds out about the 19th-century process that revolutionised photography, Diane visits a grand country estate that was transformed into a military hospital during the First World War

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With autumn on the horizon, cameras capture the seasonal planting of bulbs for spring-flowering begins at Longmeadow.. Frances Tophill meets a gardener whose allotment is filled with both traditional and exotic vegetables and Toby Buckland visits The Newt in Somerset, to find out about its chequered garden history and to explore its emergence as one of the UK's most exciting gardens. Plus, another chance to see Mark Lane's visit to Oxfordshire to see how ornamental grasses, perennials and annuals can be used in planting design, to create exciting combinations

programme.title: The Romantics and Us with Simon Schama
programme.desc: The historian recounts the story of how the Romantics - fuelled by the failures of the French Revolution and horrors of the Industrial Revolution - retreated from the tragic uproar of public life to take a journey into their own minds. Here, long before Freud and Jung and the modern psychological revolution, they discovered the subconscious - something that would have a profound effect on the modern world. Contributors include Tobias Menzies, Peter Doherty, Piotr Anderszewski and David Attenborough

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: Despite being fully aware of his limited knowledge of all things botanical, the comedian becomes a florist in Porthcawl. Cutting his teeth during the crazy Valentine's rush, Rhod has days to master this creative skill, before the business competes in the heats of the Chelsea Flower Show

programme.title: State of the Union
programme.desc: Comedy written by Nick Hornby and directed by Stephen Frears, about a couple who fear their marriage is failing and decide to give counselling sessions a try. Starring Chris O'Dowd and Rosamund Pike

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A water shortage prompts Adama to order the temporary release of prisoners to gather supplies from a deserted ice moon. However, a mutiny results in Galactica officers held hostage by a charismatic rebel questioning the legitimacy of Roslin's government and demanding free elections to be held

programme.title: Battlestar Galactica
programme.desc: A tragic accident on the Galactica kills 20 pilots, prompting Adama to order a new squad to be trained by Kara, who is still wracked with guilt over the death of the Commander's son years previously. With Mary McDonnell, Katee Sackhoff and Jamie Bamber

programme.title: Panorama
programme.desc: Investigating one of the world's most brutal trades - the buying and selling of human organs. The programme meets the African migrants who have been exploited for their body parts by criminal gangs. Some have agreed to sell a kidney to finance their journey to Europe, only to be ripped off by the traffickers after the operation. Other victims have their organs taken without consent. Panorama also tracks down the criminals running the trade to find out how they arrange illegal operations in hospitals and clinics

programme.title: Fighting the Power: Britain After George Floyd
programme.desc: Daniel Henry follows the Black Lives Matter campaign in the UK, as the country is forced to look again at its record on race in the wake of the death of George Floyd in the US

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: The Dengineers
programme.desc: The team plans to build a 'magical library' den for 10-year-old Eleanor, who has cerebral palsy, and struggles to make it to the local library to indulge her passion for books

programme.title: Wild & Weird
programme.desc: Tim Warwood and Naomi Wilkinson introduce another collection of weird clips from the natural world, this time featuring a volcano with blue lava

programme.title: All Over the Place
programme.desc: The most unusual places in the UK

programme.title: Blue Peter
programme.desc: The team presents an attempt to break a world record and a frisbee golf challenge

programme.title: Deadly Dinosaurs with Steve Backshall
programme.desc: The presenter goes on the hunt for the most deadly animals to have ever walked the earth

programme.title: Wonders of the Universe
programme.desc: Professor Brian Cox explores how the development of mankind and the history of the universe are intertwined, visiting dramatic locations around the world to illustrate the fundamental scientific principles that govern the laws of nature. He begins by investigating time, charting the evolution of the cosmos and explaining why the universe must one day come to an end

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The remarkable story of Sudan, the very last male northern white rhino on the planet. Snatched from his mother's side in Central Africa, Sudan became a prized exhibit in a zoo while the rest of his kind were poached to near-extinction. This programme focuses on the desperate battle to bring his sub-species back from the brink of extinction, told through the characters who have been involved in his life

programme.title: This Farming Life
programme.desc: The farmers prepare for the worst of the winter weather, and disaster strikes for Helen and Graham when one of their Highland cows contracts a chronic, contagious wasting disease. In the Northumberland National Park, Emma and Ewan prepare for a sheepdog trial, while in Cumbria, Frank and his daughter Georgia acquire a small herd of pygmy goats. Joyce travels to Edinburgh to give a speech to mark the launch of the Women in Agriculture report at the Royal Highland and Agriculture headquarters

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King continue their culinary journey around Asia by heading to Thailand, where they discover why Bangkok has a reputation as the street food capital of the world, and help serve up school dinners. Heading out into the country's central plains, the duo join paddy-field workers and find out how rice is grown

programme.title: Hairy Bikers Short
programme.desc: The food enthusiasts cook traditional pasties at Geevor Tin Mine in Cornwall, then head northwards to Melton Mowbray, where they try their hand at making pork pies. The duo's trip ends on the banks of the River Deveron in Scotland, where they cook salmon coulibiac - a whole fish stuffed with prawns wrapped in filo pastry - for Highland dancers and pipers

programme.title: My Life on a Plate
programme.desc: Colin Jackson invites chef Brian Turner to accompany him to Cardiff's fresh food market, where his mother used to take him to as a child, and he still shops at today. Jackson's culinary upbringing combined the staples of Caribbean cuisine with produce from the local larder and Brian finds plenty to inspire him as he sets about creating a dish full of nostalgia for the former professional athlete

programme.title: Holiday of My Lifetime with Len Goodman
programme.desc: The Strictly judge takes TV presenter Dan Walker back to Aberdeen to reminisce about a memorable childhood holiday he enjoyed there in 1991

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Six years ago Kim Selby was struck down by a serious illness. Having pulled through, she now wants to move to Australia, but not all her family are as keen. Nicki Chapman presents

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: At Swanage lifeboat station, brothers Matt and Gavin, following in their father's footsteps, race to rescue a pair of novice paddleboarders before they are swept out into the busy shipping lanes. On the north east coast, the Withernsea crew scours the North Sea for a local fisherman and friend who is missing at sea, while in Blackpool, a volunteer's wedding day is interrupted by an urgent call for help

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Hull FC v Wigan Warriors (Kick-off 5.00pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at AJ Bell Stadium, as the line-up for the semi-finals is confirmed. Wigan have lifted this trophy on 19 occasions, but the most recent of those triumphs was in 2013, while the Airlie Birds won the competition back-to-back in 2015 and 2016. With analysis from Jamie Jones-Buchanan and Jon Wilkin and commentary by Dave Woods

programme.title: Britain's Biggest Dig
programme.desc: Professor Alice Roberts and Dr Yasmin Khan investigate the excavation of a vast Georgian cemetery near Euston Station to make way for the HS2 rail link. They discover what the site reveals about the lives of the people who were buried there, from the founders of Christie's auction house to a small child who was accidentally buried alive

programme.title: Earth's Tropical Islands
programme.desc: David Harewood narrates a documentary exploring three of the most exotic and remote islands on the planet, beginning with the unique and extraordinary wildlife of Madagascar. As the oldest island on Earth, life has had time to evolve and there are now more unique plants and animals on Madagascar than any other, with footage of ring-tailed lemurs, labord's chameleons and Decken's sifakas

programme.title: QI
programme.desc: Joe Lycett, Phil Wang and Ellie Taylor join regular panellist Alan Davies, as questionmaster Sandi Toksvig looks at the pastimes of past times. As ever, correctness and even intelligence go out of the window as the panellists attempt to answer the fiendishly difficult questions, with points invariably awarded for the answers the questionmaster finds most interesting, as opposed to correct

programme.title: I, Tonya (2017)
programme.desc: Ice skater Tonya Harding rises through the ranks at the US Figure Skating Championships, but her future in the sport is thrown into question when her ex-husband steps in. Director Craig Gillespie's comedy biopic, starring Margot Robbie (who also produced the movie), Sebastian Stan, and Allison Janney, who won a Bafta and an Oscar for her performance

programme.title: Geraint Thomas: The Road Will Decide
programme.desc: Documentary following the 2018 Tour de France winner as he battled to retain the title. The film captures the intensity of his journey as he negotiates 21 stages and 3,500km in 23 days, while facing extreme weather conditions, cancelled stages and competitors such as France's Julian Alaphilippe

programme.title: Jimi: All Is By My Side (2013)
programme.desc: Biopic of Jimi Hendrix following the events of 1966, when he left New York to spend a year living in London. The relationships he developed over that time would shape his career as a musician, culminating in his famous appearance at the Monterey Pop Festival. Starring Andre Benjamin, Hayley Atwell and Imogen Poots. The film does not include any songs written by Hendrix, as the film-makers' request to use them was denied by Experience Hendrix LLC (Hendrix's estate). Instead, the film includes songs that Hendrix performed during those years, shortly before the release of his debut album Are You Experienced

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: A to Z of TV Gardening
programme.desc: Carol Kirkwood looks at subjects beginning with the letter H, visiting the Hyacinth National Collection, before Monty Don demonstrates how to plant the bulbs. Plus, a look at keeping hens

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Margherita Taylor are in Stoke-on-Trent to learn about an ambitious scheme to bring the countryside into the heart of the city. Margherita finds out what it takes to move a stretch of the River Trent, while Matt meets the next generation of nature lovers as he glories in mud with some of the city's youngest residents and their parents. In the light of the coronavirus pandemic, Tom Heap investigates whether the UK is sufficiently armed against further threats of diseases that spread from animals to humans, and Adam Henson meets another set of regional competitors vying to be crowned national One Man and His Dog champion

programme.title: Gardeners' World
programme.desc: With autumn on the horizon, cameras capture the seasonal planting of bulbs for spring-flowering begins at Longmeadow.. Frances Tophill meets a gardener whose allotment is filled with both traditional and exotic vegetables and Toby Buckland visits The Newt in Somerset, to find out about its chequered garden history and to explore its emergence as one of the UK's most exciting gardens. Plus, another chance to see Mark Lane's visit to Oxfordshire to see how ornamental grasses, perennials and annuals can be used in planting design, to create exciting combinations

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Beinn Eighe National Nature Reserve in Wester Ross to find out what has changed, as visitors return to such rural areas after lockdown. He also learns how a genetically distinct ancient pine forest is being regenerated for the future. Euan McIlwraith discovers how Scotland's wildlife from Mull to Shetland is thriving this year, Arlene Stuart finds out who picked the fruit and vegetable crops this season, and Anne Lundon follows the epic journey of Glasgow's water supply

programme.title: Beechgrove
programme.desc: Carole Baxter creates a bog garden from scratch in her rural garden and George Anderson is in his allotment, where he checks on his apples and pears. Brian Cunningham puts his no-mow garden to bed for the winter and adds some sparkles of spring interest and Kirsty Wilson is in her flat looking at reviving interest in a plant that has gone out of fashion, the African violet

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Best Bites
programme.desc: Matt Tebbutt presents highlights of the series, recalling his favourite moments from previous years in the kitchen

programme.title: Mary Berry's Simple Comforts
programme.desc: Mary Berry explores the world of simple comfort food, demonstrating how to make heartwarming, fuss-free dishes that can warm the cockles and lift the spirits. In the second episode, Mary takes a journey along the Thames in London to discover culinary hidden gems that inspire her to make some deliciously comforting dishes. Her journey starts with the Henley Rowing Club, where she rustles up mixed bean and butternut squash wraps for the team, before heading to The Waterside Inn, the world famous three Michelin Star restaurant run by the Roux family. Later, Mary discovers an oasis floating on the Thames, when she meets an established river community who have turned their barges into gardens teaming with fruit and vegetables

programme.title: The Hairy Bikers' Asian Adventure
programme.desc: Dave Myers and Si King conclude their culinary journey around Thailand by travelling to the beaches of the south and the mountains of the north to discover how the country's multicultural history and its place at the heart of the spice trade created a rich and varied cuisine. They meet a coconut-picking monkey, head out to sea during a monsoon for a nerve-racking fishing expedition and cook a curry for a remote hill tribe

programme.title: Best Bakes Ever
programme.desc: Actress and cake expert Jane Asher presents a selection of baking recipes from TV chefs, including James Martin, Mary Berry, Paul Hollywood, the Hairy Bikers, Raymond Blanc, Nigella Lawson, Tom Kerridge, Lorraine Pascale and Rachel Khoo

programme.title: Royal Recipes
programme.desc: Michael Buerk and chef Anna Haugh sample a luxurious dish originally created for Charles II, and explore the ingredients that went into one lavish dish at a royal banquet

programme.title: Stories of Us
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley and Ashley John-Baptiste learn about controlling emotions, discovering how adrenaline-packed activities can be a root to inner peace

programme.title: Doctor Zhivago (1965)
programme.desc: A married Russian physician falls in love with another woman, but as their passionate affair plays out against the raging backdrop of the First World War and the Bolshevik revolution, he is torn between his desires and his loyalty to his wife. David Lean's epic drama based on Boris Pasternak's novel, starring Omar Sharif, Julie Christie, Geraldine Chaplin, Rod Steiger, Alec Guinness, Ralph Richardson, Tom Courtenay and Klaus Kinski

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Philip Serrell and Charles Hanson appraise a chair made from the wood of Lord Nelson's ship while visiting the National Maritime Museum in Falmouth, Cornwall

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Jay Blades, Shaun Keaveny, Rachel Parris and Jan Ravens go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of entertaining trivia-based games, vying to be crowned weekly champion

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Sara Davies, Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman and Tej Lalvani consider the profit-making potential of more business ideas. A German-Portuguese partnership proposes a new kind of camera lens for mobile phones, a lifelong baker shares her recipe for vegan treats, and a third team pitches their idea for intravenous vitamins. Evan Davis presents

programme.title: Mortimer & Whitehouse: Gone Fishing
programme.desc: Paul Whitehouse and Bob Mortimer go fishing for crucian carp in Wiltshire, a notoriously elusive fish that is usually only sighted at either dusk or dawn. They spend the night in a deconsecrated church, where Paul shares a new song that he is working on, while Bob brings a new fishing chair to the river that Paul does not approve of

programme.title: Harry Hill's World of TV
programme.desc: The comedian explores the history of cookery shows, taking a look back at memorable moments and mishaps from programmes featuring Fanny Cradock, Delia Smith, Mary Berry, Jamie Oliver and Ainsley Harriott. He also recalls the highlights of MasterChef and The Great British Bake Off, and reveals why innuendo is an essential element of a great cooking show

programme.title: Louis Theroux: Life on the Edge
programme.desc: The film-maker looks back on his documentaries about the criminal justice system in the US. Louis reflects on his reports on the prison system, which exposed a world with its own rules and codes and a system that seemed to be broken. He also revisits the documentaries he shot in Philadelphia and Milwaukee, where tensions between the African-American community and the police were already at boiling point years before the death of George Floyd

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam has to remind Frankie that she knows more about boys and men than her daughter, and Duke has to make a choice between recreational activities

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam falls in love with New Orleans when she is invited to her friends' wedding, but has mixed feelings when an ex-lover seems to be everywhere she goes, with his new girlfriend

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Matt's private security business gets off to a rough start when he struggles to deal with an abrasive client. Life at home deteriorates for Claire, while Nathan confronts a secret from his past and Suresh finds another person on the list. Drama, starring Hayden Panettiere and Sendhil Ramamurthy

programme.title: Heroes
programme.desc: Peter is shocked to discover he and Claude may have been betrayed by someone close to them, and Hiro is forced to make a decision about the mission. Matt's world is thrown into chaos by the arrival of a woman with strange mental abilities, and Claire struggles to deal with her anger. Milo Ventimiglia stars

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate, inviting a panel of politicians and other guests to answer questions from viewers at home on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week

programme.title: Reported Missing
programme.desc: Just six weeks after his wedding, a 28-year-old man named Darren disappears, leaving Inspector Simon Orton and the team at Durham Police baffled. As the detectives dig deeper into the events leading up to his disappearance, they uncover a series of secrets and lies, and begin to realise all is not what it seems. Clare McElvaney and her partner from South Durham CID have to work out which information they have been given is relevant to their case, and which is not

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. Barry White's deep baritone also put both black and white audiences 'in the mood'. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Classic Soul at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances from the BBC archive by acclaimed soul singers, including Aretha Franklin, Al Green, Isaac Hayes, Dusty Springfield, Solomon Burke and Percy Sledge

programme.title: Jimi Hendrix: The Road to Woodstock
programme.desc: A definitive account of one of the guitarist's most celebrated performances, at the New York festival in 1969, featuring signature renditions of Purple Haze, Voodoo Child and the Star Spangled Banner. The programme includes interviews with Woodstock promoter Michael Lang and Hendrix's band members Mitch Mitchell, Billy Cox, Larry Lee and Juma Sultan

programme.title: Gregory Porter's Popular Voices
programme.desc: The soul and jazz performer examines how early 20th-century blues growlers, such as Bessie Smith, paved the way for the rhyme and flow of hip-hop, how truth became a quest of rock 'n' roll's greatest poets from Woody Guthrie to Gil Scott-Heron, to Lou Reed and Suzanne Vega, and why popular voices including Bob Dylan, Leonard Cohen and Kurt Cobain don't have to be technically perfect to resonate so deeply and stir people's souls. Contributors include Dave Grohl, Suzanne Vega and KRS-One

programme.title: Soul America
programme.desc: How, in the '70s and '80s, a second coming of soul men appealed to black women. New record label Philadelphia International was born that would also soften soul's hard edges. It was home to Harold Melvin and the Blue Notes, saw the emergence of Teddy Pendergrass and incorporated the O'Jays, who embodied a message of love and togetherness. Marvin Gaye went to work on his follow-up to What's Going On, shifting focus from the streets to the bedroom with Let's Get It On. Barry White's deep baritone also put both black and white audiences 'in the mood'. Narrated by Carleen Anderson

programme.title: Classic Soul at the BBC
programme.desc: Performances from the BBC archive by acclaimed soul singers, including Aretha Franklin, Al Green, Isaac Hayes, Dusty Springfield, Solomon Burke and Percy Sledge

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. The programme examines how to read cats' enigmatic facial expressions, and studies the unusual behaviour of domestic pets that have turned feral

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin leaves eastern Sudan behind and embarks on an arduous trek through Ethiopia, a country where political upheavals are evident and the threat of bandits is all too real. The tension eases once he reaches the Kenyan border, however, as the intrepid traveller turns his attention to procuring a new suit of safari gear

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: A decapitated corpse and drug-running bring taciturn detective Jay Swan to the remote, tight-lipped coastal community of Gideon

programme.title: Mystery Road
programme.desc: Jay suspects the trucking firm is tied to the drug trade, but the intervention of an ex-military businessman also proves intriguing

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: The Beach: Isolation in Paradise
programme.desc: Film-maker Warwick Thornton gives up life in the fast lane - at least for a while - to go it alone on an isolated beach and see if he can transform and heal his life

programme.title: Clive James: Postcard from London
programme.desc: The Australian journalist and presenter assesses how the city has changed over the decades, with contributions from Michael Caine, Victoria Wood, Terence Donovan and Peter Cook. First broadcast in 1991

programme.title: Top of the Pops: 1978
programme.desc: A selection of classic songs from 1978, a year in which punk music and new wave rebellion co-existed with pop, disco, soul, reggae and prog rock. Featuring appearances on the show by Ian Dury, Elvis Costello, Blondie, Bob Marley, the Boomtown Rats, Kate Bush, Sham 69, Brotherhood of Man, Dan Hill, Boney M, 10cc and Manfred Mann's Earth Band

programme.title: Pole to Pole
programme.desc: Michael Palin leaves eastern Sudan behind and embarks on an arduous trek through Ethiopia, a country where political upheavals are evident and the threat of bandits is all too real. The tension eases once he reaches the Kenyan border, however, as the intrepid traveller turns his attention to procuring a new suit of safari gear

programme.title: How We Tamed the Cat and Dog
programme.desc: Part two of two. Documentary examining archaeology and genetics to answer questions about cats, including where they came from and how they ended up living with humans. The programme examines how to read cats' enigmatic facial expressions, and studies the unusual behaviour of domestic pets that have turned feral

programme.title: Natural World
programme.desc: The natural history documentary follows the work of tough Australian Chris 'Brolga' Barnes, who has dedicated his life to rescuing and raising orphaned kangaroos in the outback near Alice Springs. This two-part film follows Brolga as he expands operations by opening his doors to a whole new bunch of animals, including a trio of camels, a southern hairy-nosed wombat and three emu chicks, as well as building a wildlife hospital

programme.title: The Ghan: Australia's Greatest Train Journey
programme.desc: Documentary exploring the railway that runs 2979 kilometres from one side of the country to the other, beginning in suburban Adelaide and terminating in the tropical coastal town of Darwin. The route took 127 years to construct, with the work carried out by local Aboriginal surveyors and early immigrants, including the famous Afghan camel drivers, after whom the train is named. To complete the route in its entirety takes 54 hours, and includes a lengthy journey through the Outback and Australia's red centre. The programme examines how the railway led to the development of many central Australian towns, including Port Augusta and Alice Springs

programme.title: Own the Sky: Jet Pack Dreamers
programme.desc: The story of Australian David Mayman, whose passion for the tantalising possibilities of jetpacks became an obsession. Filmed over 10 years, the documentary follows his seemingly impossible quest to realise his childhood dream to build and fly the world's first jetpack - an ambition that almost cost him his life

programme.title: Ultimate Cover Versions at the BBC
programme.desc: The pop cover has proved a remarkably imaginative and durable form, and this 60-year compilation tracks this pop alchemy at its finest and most intriguing through material sought from the Corporation's archive. Footage includes artists as diverse as the Moody Blues, Soft Cell, Alexandra Burke, Mariah Carey and UB40 scoring chart hits that are, in some cases, perhaps even better than the original

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
programme.desc: The Cookie Monster pays a surprise visit, so Matilda Ramsay gets his help and together they set to work making a giant chocolate-chip treat. programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch
programme.desc: Matilda Ramsay and the rest of the Ramsay Bunch set off on a road trip to the American Wild West. Their first stop is a cowboy ranch in the Nevada desert. At the ranch Matilda and her brother Jack, mum Tana and dad Gordon saddle up on horseback, learn how to ride horses like cowboys and get the chance to round up cattle - Wild West style. While the rest of the Ramsay Bunch learn how to use a lasso, Matilda prepares vegetable ratatouille parcels and marinated steaks, cooked on the campfire

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch

programme.title: Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch

Matilda's in charge of the party food: build your own spicy sausage pitta bread sandwiches with zingy yoghurt sauce and popcorn cakes. The popcorn cakes idea came from Scarlet, a Matilda and the Ramsay Bunch viewer who emailed it to Matilda's Food Blog. Great food, friends and fun, and the party finishing with a bang when the guys play an explosive game with a watermelon - parties don't get much better!

programme.title: My Life
programme.desc: My Dog Ears follows the journey of 12-year-old Joe who is hoping to receive a very precious gift - a dog that will become his ears. Joe has been profoundly deaf since he was a baby and a specially trained hearing dog could bring him the independence and confidence he so craves. Only 30 children in the UK have one of these dogs and Joe is next on the waiting list, but his journey isn't straightforward. Joe also has a Titanium leg and needs an operation to help him walk before he can receive his dog. Can Joe fully recover from his surgery and complete the training that will allow him to have a new best friend - his very own hearing dog?

programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Wales Today Coronavirus Daily Update
programme.desc: The latest developments, including the daily Welsh Government news conference

programme.title: BBC Wales Today; Weather
programme.desc: News and weather from BBC Wales

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Liam Garrigan and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Wales once led the world in the production of coal, exporting the black gold to the four corners of the Earth. Since that industry collapsed much of the infrastructure has been destroyed, forgotten or hidden away. Here, the adventurer goes in search of those final pieces of coal history, visiting one of the country's last coal mines, where they have dug up centuries' old workings. Will also explores the abandoned buildings of some south Wales forgotten collieries and lands on an island that was responsible for wrecking hundreds of coal ships

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Rhod Gilbert's Work Experience
programme.desc: The comedian tries his hand at being a scout leader, and is put in charge of leading a large group of excitable 12-year-olds safely through a forest on a wilderness survival camp. The real test, however, is whether or not Rhod will be able to bite his tongue when his tent comes under attack at 7am

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Adventure, recounted through Tonto's memories of the earliest efforts from himself and the Lone Ranger to subdue local villainy and bring justice to the American Old West. Johnny Depp, Armie Hammer, Tom Wilkinson and Helena Bonham Carter star

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the second episode, Nadiya shares recipes for her most indulgent desserts, from frozen blueberry and banana cheesecake to husband's favourite jam roly poly. Later, Nadiya also shares her tips and tricks for her picture perfect tutti frutti pavlova

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury Antiques and Collectors Fair in Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. The host also pops across to Bristol to meet the maker of cigar box guitars

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v St Helens (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at AJ Bell Stadium. Saints will be looking for revenge after Warrington denied them a league and cup double with a shock 18-4 victory in last year's final. With analysis from Jamie Jones-Buchanan and Jon Wilkin and commentary by Dave Woods and Matt Newsum

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited young inventor, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly, beginning with Movies. Hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, each special will see the judges - Craig Revel Horwood, Motsi Mabuse, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli - as well all the Strictly professional dancers and special guests, reminisce about the iconic dances, the high-octane group numbers and the standout moments from the movie-themed weeks over the years

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, the contestants are Patsy Kensit, Jake Canuso, Jenny Hanley, David Hamilton, Chris Jarvis, Pui Fan Lee, Andrea McLean and Linda Robson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents, as regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by one-time Bake Off champion Nadiya Hussain, comic and actor Michael Smiley, and comedians Bob Mortimer and Diane Morgan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed the liar of the week

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. The Gunners and Red Devls both had high hopes of strong campaigns after each ending their domestic campaigns on a high last time out, and will have expected home wins. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Cleveland Browns and Cincinnati Bengals. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world

programme.title: Papadopoulos & Sons (2012)
programme.desc: A Greek millionaire loses everything he has in a financial crisis, leaving him with only one asset - his share in the fish and chip shop his father used to run. He is forced to return to his roots and work with his laidback brother to revive the family business. Comedy, starring Stephen Dillane, Georgia Groome and Georges Corraface

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather
