programme.desc: A round-up of the week's stories, featuring interviews with key figures in politics and current affairs and a round-up of the Sunday papers. Including news and weather at 10.00

programme.title: Politics Wales
programme.desc: The latest political news and debate, and reports from around Wales

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mark and Gill Johnson fly out to Perth for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved there permanently. Aged in their forties, the pair know it's either now or never, but as an only child Mark has split loyalties. Nicki Chapman follows their progress in reaching a decision

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley marks Harvest in Saddleworth, on the Yorkshire-Lancashire border, and Katie Piper meets a Christian minister helping Welsh farmers through times of crisis

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander explore a detached house in Chesterton, Staffordshire, a former police office in Netherton in the Midlands and a Victorian house in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore and her team revamp a pair of brown atomic chairs with the help of upholsterer Anthony Devine, and later find a mid-century hall unit that needs bringing up to date

programme.title: Our Lives
programme.desc: Documentary following 26-year-old Teleri Fielden, who won a scholarship to run National Trust farm Llyndy Isaf near Beddgelert in north Wales for a year. Teleri is determined to break into the male-dominated industry, but will she be able to cope with lambing on one of the toughest farms in Snowdonia during one of the bleakest springs in living memory? Part of the Our Lives documentary strand

programme.title: A Special School
programme.desc: Observational documentary following the reality of life in Britain's biggest special school. In the first episode pupils face their fears at an outdoor adventure centre. The focus of the trip is for the young people to have an opportunity to challenge their disability physically, and abseiling in a wheelchair epitomises this experience

programme.title: Hidden Wales with Will Millard
programme.desc: Wales once led the world in the production of coal, exporting the black gold to the four corners of the Earth. Since that industry collapsed much of the infrastructure has been destroyed, forgotten or hidden away. Here, the adventurer goes in search of those final pieces of coal history, visiting one of the country's last coal mines, where they have dug up centuries' old workings. Will also explores the abandoned buildings of some south Wales forgotten collieries and lands on an island that was responsible for wrecking hundreds of coal ships

programme.title: Coal House
programme.desc: The children learn how to cope with 1920s schooling and discipline, while the mothers get to grips with mangles, ranges and the first tin bath as the families continue settling into the punishing routine of a 1927 coal mining community. Nicola Reynolds narrates

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: With a big game looming against Manchester United. West Ham's leading scorer Martha Thomas is still suffering from an injury, and seeks emotional support from her girlfriend Ellie

programme.title: Landward
programme.desc: Dougie Vipond heads to Beinn Eighe National Nature Reserve in Wester Ross to find out what has changed, as visitors return to such rural areas after lockdown. He also learns how a genetically distinct ancient pine forest is being regenerated for the future. Euan McIlwraith discovers how Scotland's wildlife from Mull to Shetland is thriving this year, Arlene Stuart finds out who picked the fruit and vegetable crops this season, and Anne Lundon follows the epic journey of Glasgow's water supply

The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. But this socially-distanced shepherding showdown is as testing and competitive as ever. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. Whoever gets the highest combined score will secure the pride of their nation and the coveted title

programme.title: Match of the Day Live
programme.desc: Leicester City v Burnley (Kick-off 7.00pm). All the action from the Premier League encounter at King Power Stadium. Leicester finished in a more than credible fifth place last season, but will have been disappointed to miss out on the Champions League places as their form dipped once the league restarted. In contrast, Burnley started the campaign slowly, but finished it strongly to end the season in 10th and their defensive organisation is likely to provide the Foxes with a difficult test this evening

programme.title: Us
programme.desc: Douglas and Connie Petersen look forward to a holiday of a lifetime, touring Europe with their teenage son Albie before he leaves home for university. However, the trip soon becomes a desperate quest to save the couple's marriage. The holiday begins in Paris, where Douglas and Connie struggle to hide the tension between them from Albie, who's already less-than-thrilled to be backpacking with his parents. On the family's first night abroad, Albie exchanges an evening spent with his mum and dad for time alone on the streets of Paris with his guitar. Drama based on the novel by David Nicholls. Starring Tom Hollander, Saskia Reeves and Tom Taylor

programme.title: Match of the Day 2
programme.desc: Mark Chapman reviews the day's Premier League action, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Southampton v Tottenham Hotspur. The Blues were looking to make a statement against champions Liverpool after spending heavily in the summer, while the Saints were hoping to emulate their shock 1-0 win at home to Spurs last term. Plus, Leicester City v Burnley, Newcastle v Brighton & Hove Albion and a round-up of yesterday's goals

programme.title: Sportscene
programme.desc: Steven Thompson presents action from the latest Premiership fixtures - Hibernian v Rangers at Easter Road and Aberdeen v Motherwell at Pittodrie. With analysis from Shaun Maloney and Michael Stewart

programme.title: Squad Goals
programme.desc: With a big game looming against Manchester United. West Ham's leading scorer Martha Thomas is still suffering from an injury, and seeks emotional support from her girlfriend Ellie

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.title: Crimewatch Roadshow
programme.desc: Michelle Ackerley and Rav Wilding present appeals for information from the public about crimes around the country, with reports from John Paul Davies

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell view a chalet bungalow in East Peckham, Kent, a piece of land in Tredegar, south-east Wales, and an end-terrace in Hartshill, Warwickshire

programme.title: Ill Gotten Gains
programme.desc: Police in North Yorkshire look for county lines drug dealers in Harrogate, while at the auction one fraudster is about to have their home sold to pay back the money they made illegally. Presented by Angellica Bell and Rav Wilding

programme.title: Britain's Housing Scandal
programme.desc: Matt Allwright investigates a Surrey property overwhelmed with rubbish and talks to the owner about their struggles with hoarding. In west London, probate genealogist Richard tracks down a deceased homeowner's relatives to help them claim their inheritance

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Tim Weeks lead teams searching for antiques in Leominster, Herefordshire, to take on to auction. Presenter Christina Trevanion monitors their progress and also learns about the Leominster Ducking Stool, a punishment device used to plunge rowdy locals in the river

programme.title: BBC Newsline; Weather
programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland

programme.title: Land Girls
programme.desc: Connie tries to win Henry's respect by standing up to the overbearing bishop, and Finch is angry to learn Esther received money for Martin's operation. Fed up with the Land Army, Joyce forces Tucker into an admission of guilt, and Lady Hoxley's sister Diana is part of Jack's plan to escape with Bea and the baby. Drama, starring Seline Hizli, Liam Garrigan and Oliver Ford Davies

programme.title: Hairy Bikers' Best of British
programme.desc: Si King and Dave Myers prepare dishes from their favourite cookery books, explaining their value as social documents that reveal a great deal about people and what they ate. The duo meet Ivan Day, who has one of the most extensive collections of recipe books in the UK, and share stories of renowned food writers such as Elizabeth David and Isabella Beeton

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: A couple from Worcestershire request the services of Alistair Appleton to help them find the perfect home in Cornwall for their £650,000 budget. Away from the property hunt, Alistair also discovers a pioneering project that prescribes the therapeutic benefits of nature to improve well-being

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: Charlie Dimmock and Harry and David Rich help a Bedfordshire woman pay tribute to her late husband. Maureen wants her new garden to be a reminder of her and her Graham's life together, with a sense of their old mountain views mixed with a Moroccan-cum-Cordoba style courtyard, inspired by their travels. Can Charlie and the Rich brothers transport this blank canvas to Spain on a £3,000 budget?

programme.title: The Repair Shop
programme.desc: Precious metal expert Brenton West takes on a pewter chess set representing the War of Independence, complete with 32 figures of British and American soldiers. A Syrian chair and a wartime mascot doll are also restored. Presented by Jay Blades

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Alex Jones and Riyadh Khalaf host the usual mix of celebrity guests and stories about people in extraordinary circumstances

programme.title: This Sporting Life
programme.desc: In 1972 Mary Peters won the Olympic gold medal in the women's pentathlon in the Munich Games, becoming a hero in Northern Ireland and beyond. She reveals to Stephen Watson the story behind her win, how she defied threats and why her last medal win in 1974 was particularly poignant

programme.title: A Question of Sport
programme.desc: With Super Bowl winner Osi Umenyiora, world champion swimmer Keri-anne Payne, former England defender Stuart Pearce and ex-World Superbike champion Neil Hodgson. Presented by Sue Barker, with team captains Phil Tufnell and Matt Dawson

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray and Chantelle arrive back in Walford, but despite her finally finding the courage to tell her husband she is leaving him, Chantelle is unaware that the silent horror she has lived with for many years is about to get even worse

programme.title: Peter Kay's Stand-Up Comedy Shuffle
programme.desc: The stand-up comedian relives his finest career moments from the last 25 years, taking in his stage shows, theatre work and hit sitcoms including Phoenix Nights and Car Share

programme.title: Mrs Brown's Boys
programme.desc: A hypnotist is booked to provide the entertainment at the triplets' baptism - and Agnes soon finds herself under his spell. Meanwhile, the matriarch's kitchen is falling apart - but that turns out to be the least of her problems when son Mark delivers some heartbreaking news. Hit comedy, starring Brendan O'Carroll as the foul-mouthed mother-of-six

programme.title: The Lone Ranger (2013)
programme.desc: A lawyer is left for dead after an ambush in which his Texas ranger brother is killed, but is saved by a mysterious Native American fugitive. Concealing his identity behind a mask, he and his new ally hunt down the outlaws behind the attack - only to find their actions are just part of a larger evil scheme. Adventure, recounted through Tonto's memories of the earliest efforts from himself and the Lone Ranger to subdue local villainy and bring justice to the American Old West. Johnny Depp, Armie Hammer, Tom Wilkinson and Helena Bonham Carter star

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present national and international news, plus sports reports, weather forecasts and arts and entertainment features. Including NewsWatch at 7.45

programme.title: Saturday Kitchen Live
programme.desc: Host Matt Tebbutt is joined by chef Shivi Ramoutar, along with special guest Cat Deeley. Plus, clips of Rick Stein, Keith Floyd, Nadiya Hussain and Sam Evans

programme.title: Nadiya Bakes
programme.desc: Nadiya Hussain shares her love of baking with some of her favourite recipes, from every day treats to indulgent desserts. In the second episode, Nadiya shares recipes for her most indulgent desserts, from frozen blueberry and banana cheesecake to husband's favourite jam roly poly. Later, Nadiya also shares her tips and tricks for her picture perfect tutti frutti pavlova

programme.title: Football Focus
programme.desc: Dan Walker and guests review the week's headlines and look ahead to forthcoming fixtures, including Chelsea v Liverpool and Wolverhampton Wanderers v Manchester City

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Natasha Raskin Sharp presents from Newbury Antiques and Collectors Fair in Berkshire, where experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams acquire items of value to take to auction. The host also pops across to Bristol to meet the maker of cigar box guitars

programme.title: Live Challenge Cup Rugby League
programme.desc: Warrington Wolves v St Helens (Kick-off 2.30pm). Mark Chapman presents all the action from the quarter-final at AJ Bell Stadium. Saints will be looking for revenge after Warrington denied them a league and cup double with a shock 18-4 victory in last year's final. With analysis from Jamie Jones-Buchanan and Jon Wilkin and commentary by Dave Woods and Matt Newsum

programme.title: Final Score
programme.desc: Jason Mohammad presents a round-up of the classified results and this afternoon's news from the Premier League, EFL and Scotland

programme.title: Beauty and the Beast (2017)
programme.desc: A prince is transformed into a hideous monster as punishment for his selfishness, and can only regain his human form by finding true love. Salvation comes in the form of a spirited young inventor, who offers to take her father's place when he is imprisoned in the monster's castle. But as romance blossoms between the unlikely pair, a jealous rival suitor only sees a beast to be slain. Live action remake of the Disney animated fairytale, starring Emma Watson, Dan Stevens and Luke Evans, with Ewan McGregor, Emma Thompson and Ian McKellen playing the Beast's entourage of living ornaments

programme.title: Strictly Come Dancing
programme.desc: A selection of the nation's favourite moments from themed weeks on Strictly, beginning with Movies. Hosted by Tess Daly and Claudia Winkleman, each special will see the judges - Craig Revel Horwood, Motsi Mabuse, Shirley Ballas and Bruno Tonioli - as well all the Strictly professional dancers and special guests, reminisce about the iconic dances, the high-octane group numbers and the standout moments from the movie-themed weeks over the years

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman present as famous faces take part in the general knowledge quiz in which they try to come up with the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public. This time, the contestants are Patsy Kensit, Jake Canuso, Jenny Hanley, David Hamilton, Chris Jarvis, Pui Fan Lee, Andrea McLean and Linda Robson

programme.title: Would I Lie to You?
programme.desc: Rob Brydon presents, as regular team captains David Mitchell and Lee Mack are joined by one-time Bake Off champion Nadiya Hussain, comic and actor Michael Smiley, and comedians Bob Mortimer and Diane Morgan. As usual, the participants aim to hoodwink their opponents with absurd facts and plausible lies about themselves in a bid to secure a team victory and the dubious honour of being dubbed the liar of the week

programme.title: Match of the Day
programme.desc: Gary Lineker presents highlights of the latest Premier League matches, including Arsenal v West Ham United at Emirates Stadium and Manchester United v Crystal Palace at Old Trafford. The Gunners and Red Devls both had high hopes of strong campaigns after each ending their domestic campaigns on a high last time out, and will have expected home wins. Plus, Everton v West Bromwich Albion and Leeds United v Fulham

programme.title: The NFL Show
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Osi Umenyiora and Jason Bell to present highlights of Thursday night's game between Cleveland Browns and Cincinnati Bengals. Plus, a look at the major news stories shaping the American football world

programme.title: Papadopoulos & Sons (2012)
programme.desc: A Greek millionaire loses everything he has in a financial crisis, leaving him with only one asset - his share in the fish and chip shop his father used to run. He is forced to return to his roots and work with his laidback brother to revive the family business. Comedy, starring Stephen Dillane, Georgia Groome and Georges Corraface

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Martin Geissler and Tina Daheley present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather

programme.desc: The political developments of the week, with policy-makers questioned on the key issues

programme.title: Sunday Morning Live
programme.desc: Sean Fletcher and Sally Phillips take a look at the week's talking points and explore the ethical and religious issues of the day

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Mark and Gill Johnson fly out to Perth for a trial week to check out the property, work and lifestyle they could have if they moved there permanently. Aged in their forties, the pair know it's either now or never, but as an only child Mark has split loyalties. Nicki Chapman follows their progress in reaching a decision

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Charles Hanson and David Harper help two teams search for items at Wetherby Racecourse in West Yorkshire, while Charlie Ross challenges fairgoers to guess his mystery item

programme.title: Songs of Praise
programme.desc: The Rev Kate Bottley marks Harvest in Saddleworth, on the Yorkshire-Lancashire border, and Katie Piper meets a Christian minister helping Welsh farmers through times of crisis

programme.title: Points of View
programme.desc: Viewers' opinions on BBC shows and behind-the-scenes reports on new programmes. Presented by Tina Daheley

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Lucy Alexander explore a detached house in Chesterton, Staffordshire, a former police office in Netherton in the Midlands and a Victorian house in Berkhamsted, Hertfordshire

programme.title: Escape to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin helps a couple looking for a home in Oxfordshire on a budget of £1.5 million, and also braves the county's waters during an exhilarating attempt at punt racing

programme.title: Money for Nothing
programme.desc: Sarah Moore looks for items to upcycle at Wallyford Recycling Centre in Scotland, where she comes across a broken dining table, three fabric chairs, and a collection of lawn bowls

programme.title: Garden Rescue
programme.desc: In Cwmcarn, south Wales, Kim and Simon's steep garden is little more than three huge, muddy tiers, so Charlie Dimmock and Chelsea gold medal-winners Harry and David Rich set out to turn it into a relaxing space worthy of the area's amazing views

programme.title: Countryfile
programme.desc: Matt Baker and Charlotte Smith are with four of the UK's leading shepherds and their sheepdogs for this year's Countryfile One Man and His Dog competition, which takes place on a tough course designed to test man and dog in the grounds of historic Hardwick Hall in Derbyshire. The coronavirus means things have to look a little different this year, with just one competitor from each home nation vying for glory. The premier league of shepherding talent from England, Wales, Ireland and Scotland must battle it out over two rounds - the 'singles', where they run one dog, and the notoriously difficult 'brace', where they must work two dogs at the same time. programme.title: Good Morning Britain
programme.desc: Morning magazine featuring a lively mix of news and current affairs, plus health, entertainment and lifestyle features

programme.title: Lorraine
programme.desc: Christine Lampard presents a topical mix of entertainment, discussion and showbiz glamour as well as the latest fashion, food and celebrity gossip

programme.title: This Morning
programme.desc: Daily magazine, featuring a mix of celebrity chat, showbusiness news, lifestyle features, topical discussion, health and beauty advice and more

programme.title: Loose Women
programme.desc: Guest panellists engage in topical debate and celebrity interviews from a female perspective

programme.title: Judge Rinder
programme.desc: Cameras follow criminal barrister Robert Rinder as he takes on real-life cases in a studio courtroom

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show, inviting three pairs of shoppers to 'go wild in the aisles' as they compete in the ultimate supermarket challenge to win a £3,000 prize. New quiz rounds feature alongside the legendary trolley dashes and the most famous pastel jumpers on television

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Frank, Adam, Lesley and Leigh work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Gabby tries to escape her hurt, things start looking up for Sam, and Annie is remembered

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Abi dreads Kevin's reaction to her confession, Tracy pushes Steve to confront Leanne, and Kirk believes that Nicky is Daniel's new girlfriend

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into Newcastle's underworld to discover the identity of a murder victim found dead in the river, with no clue as to who he is apart from a distinctive drugs wrapper. She discovers the dead man had ties to some very dangerous people, many of whom may have wanted him dead, while a witness's testimony links the case to a remote farm where a murder took place 13 years previously. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: The host presents a stand-up comedy showcase, featuring sets by some of the most exciting new stand-ups in the UK. Mawaan Rizwan co-hosts the first episode, which features Kae Kurd, Flo & Joan and Sophie Duker, as well as some new material from Rob Beckett

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 19, featuring a flat 166.5km course from Bourg-en-Bresse to Champagnole. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz and Weasel set up their super-antenna in the desert. Magoo ends up beside them. He mistakes the antenna for a forest's last oak tree.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Hidden inside a robot, Fizz and Weasel hack the world's Internet server, but Magoo, shopping the sales, mistakes it for a shopping cart and takes it.

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Fizz is recording chill music to de-stress the world, so he needs quiet. Unluckily, Magoo's come over to borrow a wrench and thinks there's a leak.

programme.title: Thunderbirds Are Go
programme.desc: International Rescue are dispatched to save one of their own when the Hood traps Lady Penelope and Parker inside an underwater hyper-train loop during a high-stakes robbery. Updated remake of the classic 1960s marionette adventure series, with the voices of Rosamund Pike, Kayvan Novak, Thomas Brodie-Sangster and David Graham

programme.title: Dare Master
programme.desc: Danny's challenge will see him attempt to escape from a helicopter simulator underwater. Has he got what it takes to be submerged?

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler tries to retrieve his credit card from a cash dispenser, but ends up trapped in a bank vault. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Mr Bean: The Animated Series
programme.desc: The hapless bumbler goes on a one-man mission to clean up the town after becoming furious with the state of the streets and all the people who drop litter. Chaotic cartoon capers following the further adventures of the popular halfwit immortalised in the live-action TV series and Hollywood films. With the voice of Rowan Atkinson

programme.title: Scrambled!
programme.desc: Two hours of children's fun and entertainment

programme.title: The Tom and Jerry Show
programme.desc: Overweight Jerry starts working out on a weight machine

programme.title: Craig of the Creek
programme.desc: Grab your toilet-paper sword and follow best friends Craig, JP, and Kelsey as they explore their neighbourhood creek - a boundless, kid-run world!

programme.title: Scooby-Doo and Guess Who?
programme.desc: When Scooby and the gang head to Gotham City to visit Alfred Pennyworth, they stumble into a dangerous mystery!

programme.title: Teen Titans Go!
programme.desc: Starfire becomes friends with an ice cream scoop

programme.title: James Martin's Saturday Morning
programme.desc: James is joined in the kitchen by guests Ray Quinn, Paul Ainsworth, Atul Kochhar and Dan The Fish Man. Featuring a masterclass in chilli prawns and a food adventure to Lancashire, as well as recipes fo rsardines, mackerel and chilli beef

programme.title: Ainsley's Food We Love
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares a selection of retro dishes, including minced beef, potato and onion pie served with buttery greens, coronation chicken and a tempting pudding. Vick Hope joins Ainsley to introduce him to a spicy prawn cocktail and Brian Turner reveals why faggots deserve a comeback

programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to St Tropez, where he explores the seafood market in search of ingredients to cook aboard a yacht. James also drops in on old friend Michel Roux and they prepare a rather special barbecue using ingredients from his garden

programme.title: ITV Racing Live
programme.desc: Ed Chamberlin and Francesca Cumani present coverage of the 3.25 Mill Reef Stakes from Newbury, which follows races at 2.15 and 3.50. Plus, races from Ayr, headlined by the 3.40 Gold Cup. Analysis comes from Jason Weaver, with reports by Matt Chapman, Rishi Persad and Sally Ann Grassick, and commentary from Richard Hoiles

programme.title: Inside Animal A&E
programme.desc: The team picks up fat cat Tiger, who has a rather embarrassing problem down below, which means going on a diet, while nine-year-old greyhound Carter could have a serious underlying health problem. Nurse Charlotte has a particularly unusual patient to treat as a stray corn snake is brought in, while some cute kittens visit the mobile unit for vaccinations

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking celebrity contestants Jeff Stelling, Charlotte Hawkins and Andy Whyment to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Jeff Banks, Michelle Ackerley, Andy Crane and Brian Conley pit their wits against one of the ruthless Chasers in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the third semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Jonathan Ross' Comedy Club
programme.desc: Jonathan and co-host Mawaan Rizwan present another stand-up showcase featuring some of the best and funniest new comedians in the UK. This episode features sets from Nigel Ng, Bec Hill and Jordan Brookes, while Kerry Godliman will be trying out some new material

programme.title: Play to the Whistle
programme.desc: Host Holly Willoughby and team captains Frank Lampard and Bradley Walsh are joined by golfer Andrew Johnston, Scarlett Moffatt and comic Rob Beckett. Regulars Romesh Ranganathan and Seann Walsh also appear

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from the 20th and penultimate stage of the most prestigious race on the calendar, featuring a 36.2km individual time-trial between Lure and La Planche des Belles Filles. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: Britain's Brightest Family
programme.desc: Hosted by Anne Hegerty, this new quiz show sees sixteen families from across the country take part in a knock-out tournament to win the coveted title of Britain's Brightest Family, and a holiday of a life time. The contestants go up against each other to answer a series of questions involving literacy, numeracy, problem-solving and general knowledge skills, with each team nominating a family member to answer the questions, proving how well they all know each other's strengths and weaknesses

programme.title: ITV Nightscreen

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Martin & Roman's Sunday Best!
programme.desc: It's a family affair this week as Martin introduces a performance from his daughter Harley Moon Kemp. He and Roman also chat to singer Roachford, and author and columnist Caitlin Moran and comedian Russell Kane

programme.title: Love Your Weekend with Alan Titchmarsh
programme.desc: Poet Pam Ayres and comedian and health campaigner Ruby Wax join the presenter for his celebration of the great British countryside. Alan also meets a master stone carver and some rare breed miniature horses, and samples the best of British cheese. Plus, more from the organic halal farming family the Radwans

programme.title: All Around Britain
programme.desc: Ranvir Singh and Alex Beresford climb aboard Nelson's famous flagship HMS Victory at Portsmouth's historic dockyard as they present the best of ITV regional news programmes. Stories include the Scottish island abandoned by residents 90 years ago, the Yorkshire village that hosted what has been dubbed the worst ever music festival and the girl from Northern Ireland who has become an internet star with her thousands of baby frogs

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: Ant and Dec host the third semi-final of this year's talent contest, making a return to screens following the auditions episodes broadcast in the spring. Amanda Holden, Alesha Dixon, David Walliams and Ashley Banjo are on the judging desk, as the acts that impressed them the most return, including the golden buzzer acts - singer Fayth Ifil, comedian and singer Jon Courtney, choir Sign Along With Us, comedian Nabil Abdulrashid, and mother and daughter singing duo, Honey and Sammy. Eight acts perform in each semi-final, with two finalists selected by the judges and the votes of viewers

programme.title: Celebrity Catchphrase
programme.desc: Stephen Mulhern hosts the game show, asking celebrity contestants Jeff Stelling, Charlotte Hawkins and Andy Whyment to 'see it and say it' as they try to guess the familiar phrases hidden in animated clues. One of them will make it through to the Super Catchphrase round, where they could win £50,000 for the charity of their choice

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as four contestants pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. Mark, Steve, Grace and Charlotte work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught

programme.title: Supermarket Sweep
programme.desc: Rylan Clark-Neal hosts the supermarket-based game show in which three pairs of shoppers compete in a series of challenges to win a £3,000 prize

programme.title: James Nesbitt: A Game of Two Halves
programme.desc: The accomplished actor embarks on an epic journey to see how Soccer Aid for Unicef is helping children around the world. Since 2006, Soccer Aid has raised over £38million and James will see just how far that money goes. His mission is to take essential supplies to children living in three extreme locations - the Himalayas in Nepal, the desert surrounding the Dead Sea and a remote island in the South Pacific. For James however, the journey is one into the unknown, as he won't know where he is going on each leg of his mission until just before he leaves

programme.title: Tipping Point: Lucky Stars
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts as football star Michael Owen, Strictly Come Dancing host Tess Daly and comedian Hal Cruttenden take part in the celebrity version of the game show, in which they must answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the better their chances of collecting the £20,000 prize for their selected charity

programme.title: Family Fortunes
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo takes over hosting duties on this revamped version of the popular game show, which sees two families trying to guess the answers to survey-based questions posed to 100 people. And for anyone who hasn't seen it, the family who score the most points go through to the final round, where they play for the a £30,000 jackpot

programme.title: The Singapore Grip
programme.desc: Matthew Webb settles in and is introduced to the family but after a tour of his rubber plantation he is troubled to see that questionable practices are taking place. Joan sweeps him off to an amusement part with Monty and Ehrendorf, where Vera becomes intrigued by the newcomer, and soon he finds himself in the middle of a tug-of-war between Vera and Joan, battling for his affections. But very soon Singapore is hit by a war of a different kind. Adaptation of the novel by JG Farrell, starring Luke Treadaway, David Morrissey, Georgia Blizzard and Elizabeth Tan

programme.title: The Real 'Des': The Dennis Nilsen Story
programme.desc: Following on from the three-part drama about the serial killer Dennis Nilsen, this documentary explores the personality of the man behind the crimes - on the surface a mild-mannered civil servant who admitted to murdering up to 15 young men over a four-year period in the late 1980s and early 1980s, disposing of their bodies in the garden and down the drain. The film includes footage of an interview Nilsen gave to ITV at Albany Prison in 1992, contributions from victims' families and insights from criminologist David Wilson, who corresponded with Nilsen over several decades. Narrated by David Tennant

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Documentary going behind the scenes of the airport, finding out how its infrastructure helps staff process thousands of passengers arriving from 84 countries. Border force officer Kat decides whether to allow a holiday-maker arriving with little money in and a confusing story into the UK. Meanwhile, Customer Service Agent Mr Sho is having issues with a 400-year-old Japanese tradition

programme.title: Harbour Cops
programme.desc: Documentary following the daily work of Dyfed-Powys Police officers over the busy summer months. Dyfed-Powys is the smallest police force in the UK, but has to patrol the largest police area, their jurisdiction taking in national parks and a lengthy run of the spectacular South Wales coastline. In this first episode, officers in the seaside town of Tenby on the western side of Carmarthen Bay have to deal with rowdy holidaymakers causing trouble and track down a pensioner who has been reported missing

programme.title: Motorsport UK
programme.desc: Richard John Neil presents from Brands Hatch, featuring round four of the Dunlop Mini Challenge and round three of the Porsche Carrera Cup GB

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Coronation Street

programme.title: Vera
programme.desc: The detective is drawn into Newcastle's underworld to discover the identity of a murder victim found dead in the river, with no clue as to who he is apart from a distinctive drugs wrapper. She discovers the dead man had ties to some very dangerous people, many of whom may have wanted him dead, while a witness's testimony links the case to a remote farm where a murder took place 13 years previously. The connection brings Kenny's past back to haunt him, as he was responsible for seeing the perpetrator convicted

programme.title: ITV News at Ten
programme.desc: A fresh perspective on the major stories of the day, featuring national and international news, with reports and analysis from ITV's correspondents

programme.title: Tour de France Highlights
programme.desc: Action from stage 19, featuring a flat 166.5km course from Bourg-en-Bresse to Champagnole. Gary Imlach presents, with analysis from Chris Boardman and Peter Kennaugh, reports by Daniel Friebe and Matthew Rendell, and commentary from Ned Boulting and David Millar

programme.title: 1000 Heartbeats
programme.desc: Vernon Kay hosts the quiz show in which contestants' pulses are monitored and they must complete seven rounds before their hearts beat 1,000 times to win £25,000 - meaning they will have to stay calm under pressure or risk losing the jackpot

programme.title: Mr Magoo
programme.desc: Hidden inside a robot, Fizz and Weasel hack the world's Internet server, but Magoo, shopping the sales, mistakes it for a shopping cart and takes it.

programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott prepares a selection of retro dishes, including minced beef, potato and onion pie served with buttery greens, coronation chicken and a tempting pudding. programme.title: James Martin's French Adventure
programme.desc: The chef heads to St Tropez, where he explores the seafood market in search of ingredients to cook aboard a yacht. James also drops in on old friend Michel Roux and they prepare a rather special barbecue using ingredients from his garden

programme.title: ITV Racing Live

programme.desc: While sitting through the high school talent show, Peter becomes enraptured by the violin performance of a student and convinces Cleveland, Joe and Quagmire to join him in forming a string quartet. However, when they secure a wedding gig and find that Peter has neglected to practise, they kick him out. Meanwhile, Brian is forced into being Carter's guide dog

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: James Woods takes viewers behind the scenes at the Family Guy studios to offer a peek at a production week of the series to see what goes into the making of the show

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Taylor Swift agrees to go to a homecoming dance with Chris after he asks her via online video. However, when she starts to fall for him, her music takes a hit. Meanwhile, Peter decides to try a new profession and sees firsthand what it's like to be an Uber driver

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Francine poses as a male CIA agent to get into her husband Stan's exclusive men's club, while Steve and his friends start a 12-person boy band

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Roger rediscovers one of his personas, Ricky Spanish, and soon remembers why he is the most hated person in Langley Falls. Meanwhile, an African child who Stan and Francine sponsored shows up on their doorstep

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Rallo offers Cleveland Jr advice to help him impress a girl, but the youngster's confidence is knocked by the discovery that her boyfriend is a local rap star, so he hatches a plan to woo her by challenging his rival to a rap battle. With the voice of guest star Kanye West

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland and his son teach their family about their tradition of celebrating Black History Month, but Rallo reacts aggressively to learning about his heritage

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, London Hughes, Yang Bane and Kae Kurd go up against Lady Leshurr, Darren Harriott, Jenny Bede and Snoochie Shy in the rap and hip-hop battle. Hosted by Jordan Stephens

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Reggie Yates hosts as The Box presenters AJ Odudu, Maya Jama and Will Best face the sausage-maker from hell, a zombie scientist and a creepy birdwatcher

programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Totally Bonkers Guinness World Records
programme.desc: Matt Edmonds presents more weird and wonderful Guinness World Records, including the most vehicles to run over a person and the most eggs carried on a single hat

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: The host welcomes actress Wanda Sykes to the studio, who tells her all about Visible: Out on Television. Plus, Michael Kiwanuka performs Hero from his album Kiwanuka

programme.title: The Ellen DeGeneres Show
programme.desc: Actress Jennifer Aniston takes the reigns to guest host, talking to Will Ferrell about his film Downhill and Selena Gomez about her album Rare

programme.title: Britain's Got Talent
programme.desc: The nation's favourite TV talent show, Britain's Got Talent, returns to our screens.

programme.title: Live British Touring Car Championship
programme.desc: Coverage of the fifth round of the campaign from Thruxton, featuring the 13th, 14th and 15th races of the season. Last time out at Knockhill, Ash Sutton claimed two race wins to close the gap on Colin Turkington to just 10 points at the top of the championship standings

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: FYI Daily
programme.desc: Latest news from the world of entertainment.

programme.title: Mamma Mia! Here We Go Again (2018)
programme.desc: It's five years since Sophie invited her three possible dads to her wedding, and now most of her family are reunited as she prepares for the grand reopening of the Hotel Bella Donna. Along the way, she learns more about her mother's past. Abba-themed musical sequel, starring Amanda Seyfried, Lily James, Pierce Brosnan, Cher and Andy Garcia

programme.title: Love Island USA
programme.desc: After attending a poolside yoga class, the boys and girls are separated for a few days. Arielle Vandenburg hosts.

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Quagmire discovers the dating app Tinder, forcing Peter, Joe and Cleveland to intervene and save him from his soul-sucking hedonism. Meanwhile, Stewie discovers he has scoliosis

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: In order to 'save' Stewie from autism, Peter and Lois decide not to vaccinate him and then proceed to convince the rest of Quahog to follow suit. Stewie runs away to save himself from diseases until Sean Penn swoops in to save the day and brings vaccines to the whole town

programme.title: Family Guy
programme.desc: Peter gets locked in a library and decides to read some of the books that he failed to finish in high school, and begins to imagine Quahog residents in works of great literature including The Great Gatsby, The Adventures of Huckleberry Finn and of Mice and Men

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan grows fed up with Hayley and Jeff's lazy behaviour, so he bets them he and Francine can live on the minimum wage for a month. Steve and Roger undertake a mission to test-drive their dream car

programme.title: American Dad!
programme.desc: Stan discovers that Francine was unfaithful to him before they got married, leaving him worried that Hayley might not be his biological daughter. He travels back in time to learn the identity of her father, but when he inadvertently disrupts past events, he must do everything in his power to avoid changing the future

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland takes Donna to New York for their belated honeymoon, but allows the Stoolbend gang to tag along in an effort to help Coach McFall rekindle a romance. Things worsen when Tim finds himself in a compromising position, and Lester's language gets him into trouble

programme.title: The Cleveland Show
programme.desc: Cleveland is wounded when he and Donna are mugged while using a cash point, and Roberta decides to take advantage of younger brother Rallo, who thinks she is dying of a mystery illness

programme.title: Don't Hate the Playaz
programme.desc: Maya Jama, London Hughes, Nathan Caton and Not3s go up against Lady Leshurr, Darren Harriott, Ivo Graham and Yungen in the rap and hip-hop battle. Hosted by Jordan Stephens

programme.title: Release the Hounds
programme.desc: Celebrity version of the horror game show, beginning with Towie's Bobby Cole Norris, Pete Wicks and Chloe Sims facing demented bin men, evil toys and an army of clowns

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Jim apologises to Bill for upsetting Maureen. Raquel goes for a job interview in Manchester. Tricia tells Jamie that Carl thumped her when she told him she was pregnant.

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Alf celebrates his 70th birthday. Tricia tries to make Jamie see they are better off without Carl. Raquel is furious when Curly lets her down over an evening out

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Biff urges Jan to think again about taking Kim's job offer. Zak tries to explain to Terry why he went missing the night before. Chris and Rachel celebrate their divorce

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Kelly refuses to tell Viv and Vic why she has come home. Kim calls a meeting for all her staff. Biff summons up the courage to go to the hospital for his test results

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: First of a two-part storyline. Jenny's life starts to spiral out of control and begins to affect her work, leaving Dennis and Liz facing difficult decisions. New doctor Helen Trent arrives in Aidensfield with her wealthy husband Matt, hoping to make a fresh start, but finds it hard to adjust to the slow pace of life in the country. Sophie Ward joins the regular cast, including Sarah Tansey, Duncan Bell and Aislin McGuckin

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: When Sid receives a bonus, the family are quick to remind him how helpful they all are

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is left two large packing cases in an uncle's will - but they come with a terrible curse

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George decides to place his father in a residential home, but the old man would rather stay with his son. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: The apparent accidental death of a drug addict takes a surprising turn when the results of the post-mortem examination are revealed, and Jack finds himself confronting his own chequered past. Ewen Bremner guest-stars, with David Jason

programme.title: Heartbeat
programme.desc: Conclusion of a two-part storyline. A kidnap case leaves Vernon's plans for Liz and Ben's wedding in chaos, while Helen's dreams of a fresh start for her and Matt look set to be shattered. Jenny is relieved when she finally gets a medical diagnosis explaining her recent symptoms, but the news brings a difficult decision for husband Dennis, who has to choose between his wife and his police career. Starring newcomer Sophie Ward, Oliver Montgomery, Duncan Bell and Sarah Tansey

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Ned is upset that Jan knew about Biff's possible illness. Sophie admits to Zoe that she is scared of Butch. Tony gets the blackmail money back from Adlington

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Zak is worried about Butch as he did not come home the night before. PC Wilson assesses the damage to Zoe's living room. Chris sees Kim and Steve kissing in her bedroom

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Maud urges Maureen to make peace with Bill. Alf demands Audrey cancels the health farm trip. Curly is angry to discover Raquel is trying to sort out his problems with the Dealers

programme.title: Classic Coronation Street
programme.desc: Tricia gets a letter from Carl blaming her for him losing his parole. Sue calls on Ken and opens her heart to him about her husband leaving

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: Al welcomes his first fishing guests, but an uninvited one means that a good review is definitely not guaranteed, while Bert decides to try to rejuvenate trade at the restaurant by joining an online voucher scheme. Morwenna asks Martin for a pay rise, and Louisa has to find a new babysitter to look after James Henry and reluctantly decides to offer Janice a trial. Penhale is excited to receive his new police issue Taser, but Portwenn does not exactly offer him plenty of opportunities to try it out. Comedy drama, starring Martin Clunes

programme.title: Doc Martin
programme.desc: The irascible medic decides to move out of the surgery to give Louisa space, leaving Mrs Tishell concerned he will not be able to look after himself on his own. Elsewhere, PC Penhale helps Janice babysit, and Al is very concerned when he finds out about Bert's behaviour and hears there are bailiffs at the restaurant

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Laura's resolve to bring Andrew to justice deepens and she enlists Tom's help in her plan. However, when a deception in her personal life comes to light, she is left with no one to turn to and resorts to dangerous methods to force Andrew's confession. But she is not alone in her mission. Thriller, starring Joanne Froggatt, Ioan Gruffudd and Warren Brown

programme.title: Liar
programme.desc: Terrified of being arrested, Andrew enlists Luke's help to set up an alibi, but Laura does not give up easily and the final confrontation between the two may have violent consequences. The concluding part of Harry and Jack Williams' thriller, which followed the lives of teacher Laura Nielson and widowed surgeon Andrew Earlham after they found themselves entangled in a web of deceit and confusion after enjoying a date together. Starring Joanne Froggatt and Ioan Gruffudd, with Danny Webb and Shelley Conn

programme.title: A Touch of Frost
programme.desc: A confrontation between hunt stewards and saboteurs leads to the suspicious death of a protester - and makes Jack wonder whether the thrill of the chase has given the horsey set a taste for human blood. Whodunit, starring David Jason as DI Frost, with Bruce Alexander, Sean Pertwee, Del Henney and Tat Whalley

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sally and Mike bring home a tramp, much to Sid's disgust. Comedy, starring Sid James

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid revolts against the chairman of the local residents association

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The dapper Belgian sleuth goes on an Egyptian cruise on board an old steamer, but when an American heiress is murdered, his little grey cells go into overdrive. On questioning each of his fellow passengers, he realises many of them have secrets they want to remain hidden. David Suchet stars, with James Fox, Judy Parfitt, Emily Blunt, Daisy Donovan, Frances de la Tour and David Soul

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Long-running drama with the residents of England's most famous cobbled street

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: The detective investigates after a botanist accidentally unearths the body of a recently buried professor who was fixated upon solving a seemingly impossible riddle by Lewis Carroll. The victim had a long-standing rivalry with his brother - giving Lewis and Hathaway an obvious suspect - but the case is hindered by the mind games of two students who seem intent on causing trouble in their quest to gain admittance to a mysterious club. Guest starring Celia Imrie and James Fleet, with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Local boxer John Kinsella's successful world title bid stirs up hidden passions among the residents of Midsomer Morchard. As plans to stage a celebratory re-enactment of a Victorian prizefight shape up, jealousy, betrayal and blackmail take a ringside seat, while two suspicious deaths suggest Barnaby has let friendship cloud his judgement. Detective drama, guest starring Phil Daniels (Quadrophenia, EastEnders), with John Nettles and Jason Hughes

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A planning dispute leads to murder after a stalwart in the Midsomer Conservation Society suspects her neighbours are involved in a money-making development scam. Political wrangling, burglaries and sexual liaisons abound in picturesque Great Pelfe as Barnaby and Jones investigate. Guest starring Hugo Speer (Bleak House), with John Nettles, Jason Hughes and Jane Wymark

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: DCI Tom Barnaby reluctantly joins wife Joyce on a spa weekend at Swavely Manor, a country house offering treatments to soothe the mind and body. However, as he attempts to unwind, a woman is found dead in a flotation chamber and he promptly abandons his therapy to investigate - but the body count soon rises. The detective eventually wraps up the case, but it proves to be his last as he makes a big decision about his future. John Nettles' final appearance in the long-running series, with guest stars Ronni Ancona, Geraldine James and Lesley Manville

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A walk in the woods leads to tragedy for Emily Simpson, who witnesses something unsettling before being found dead in her cottage by old friend Lucy Bellringer. Convinced natural causes are not to blame, Lucy calls in DCI Barnaby and Sergeant Troy, whose investigation eventually uncovers sinister long-buried secrets. The first ever episode of the long-running detective drama, guest starring Jessica Hynes and Emily Mortimer

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Keen to unwind, Geordie and Will head to a charity boxing match at Vic's gym. However, as Matthew and Luke face off in the ring the bout takes an unexpected twist. Will wonders if he's misjudged Matthew all along and when two boys are later found near death, Will must face up to his own part in the tragedy to help uncover the truth. Meanwhile, Leonard tries to reunite the warring Chapmans, who seem determined never to see eye-to-eye again. Robson Green, Tom Brittney and Al Weaver star

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: When a woman is found dead laid out in the shape of a crucifix on Jesus Green, Will and Geordie are led to a nearby convent where they meet steely Sister Grace, who rules over her flock with an iron fist. Even more sinister is the picture that starts to emerge as they match the nuns to a series of unsolved missing persons cases from the previous five years. Meanwhile, when Leonard's father, Russell, arrives in Grantchester, it's clear there's no love lost between parent and son. Robson Green, Tom Brittney, Tracy-Ann Oberman and Sean Gilder star

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective and his team find themselves investigating the murder of one of their own. Cabbot is convinced there is a link between the deaths of Lucy Payne and her colleague, but is dismissed as trying to push her career ambitions. However, after receiving new information from another force, she begins to uncover the real connection between the killings. Andrea Lowe stars

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective is drawn into the criminal underworld of Soho when he reluctantly accepts an unofficial request from Chief Superintendent Rydell to find his missing daughter. Meanwhile, an armed robbery in Eastvale turns into a murder investigation after the perpetrator is found dead in his home and when a battered body is found in a local nightclub, the identity of the victim leads Banks and Cabbot to a devastating realisation about both cases

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. A surveillance operation establishes a clear link between the murders of Charles McKay and Barry Clough and the subsequent investigation takes a number of alarming twists before uncovering a conspiracy pointing to one of the team being an informer. As Banks pushes all the suspects hard, the case threatens to undermine his integrity as an officer and brings his relationship with Cabbot to breaking point

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The sleuth is asked to investigate the disappearance of a bank's most valued customer, who had been planning to sell the chairman a map showing where to find a long-lost silver mine. David Suchet stars

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: The couple go to a family wedding, but they don't bargain on Jean's cousin Peggy being there

programme.title: ITV3 Nightscreen
programme.desc: Text-based information service

programme.title: Teleshopping
programme.desc: Buying goods from the comfort of home

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid has problems when Jean pleads for a new freezer

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Jean and Sally interpret Sid's dreams and Trevor tries to recreate the atmosphere of the previous night

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Billy reports his suspicions to the police, David hears from his house's insurers and Toyah answers tough questions concerning her past

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Long-running soap following the ups and downs of village life amid the rolling scenery of the Yorkshire Dales

programme.title: Lewis
programme.desc: A professor feels humiliated when her internet dating video is leaked onto a website and watched by her students. The next morning she is found dead, the victim of what appears to be a straightforward suicide. However, Lewis is not convinced and digs deeper, identifying several suspects including the site's founder - but as he and Hathaway get closer to the truth amid a rising death toll, they find their personal and professional lives dredged up online for all to see. Guest starring Roxanne McKee (Game of Thrones) and Toby Stephens (Die Another Day), with Kevin Whately and Laurence Fox

programme.title: Carry On Cruising (1962)
programme.desc: The captain of a cruise ship bound for the Mediterranean is less than impressed when five of his regular shipmates are replaced at the last minute. Despite their best efforts to earn his respect and an eagerness to please, the new recruits seem doomed to cause chaos whatever they do. Comedy, starring Sid James, Kenneth Williams, Liz Fraser and Kenneth Connor

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: Barnaby visits a new age commune, where a man has fallen to his death, or may have been pushed. The dead man turns out to have a connection to the leader's shady past - they set the commune up together as a confidence trick, but the leader came to believe in what he preaches. A further death raises greater suspicion, especially since a vast inheritance might be coming the group's way. Guest starring Stephen Moyer (True Blood)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: A prestigious golf club is the setting for murder when a player is bludgeoned to death on the 13th hole. Barnaby and Jones discover gambling, illegal money-lending and assault are rife among the snobbish members, who are also dismissive of the local villagers and only allow them on the course for a few hours a week. Before long, another body turns up, making the detectives doubly determined to catch this terror of the fairways. John Nettles and Jason Hughes star, with guest appearances by Nicholas Le Prevost, John Standing and real-life golfer Sam Torrance

programme.title: Inside the Crown: Secrets of the Royals
programme.desc: The documentary explores how the Queen has responded in times of sadness and grief, as she faced personal tragedies and national disasters including the Aberfan disaster, the Grenfell fire, and the IRA's murder of Lord Mountbatten. The Queen also faced her so-called 'Annus Horribilis' in 1992, when family scandal, press ridicule and the catastrophic Windsor Castle fire tested her skills as monarch and mother to the limit, and, of course the death of Diana, Princess of Wales, when her slow response to the nation's grief threatened to destabilise the monarchy

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: A dentist falls in love with his young assistant. His anxious wife, believing he has made plans to poison her and thereby remove the main obstacle to happiness, calls for Poirot's help. Drama, starring David Suchet

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part one of two. The detective receives a disturbing phone message from estranged brother Roy and heads to Harrogate to find him. Meanwhile at a murder scene, the team is introduced to new recruit Helen Morton, who quickly alienates her colleagues with her tactless style. The officer then makes matters worse when she finds evidence linking Banks to the victim's body and speculates he could be a suspect. Unaware everyone is searching for him, the DCI begins to delve into Roy's life and soon uncovers questionable business transactions and associates. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson, with Caroline Catz (Doc Martin) and Andrea Lowe

programme.title: DCI Banks
programme.desc: Part two of two. The detective visits his childhood home to break the news of Roy's death, and his parents plead with him to find out what happened. Defying Morton, Banks returns to the station in an attempt to lead the investigation into the murders of his brother and Jennifer Lewis, and both officers are convinced the key to cracking the cases lies with a woman being treated at a clinic. Crime drama, starring Stephen Tompkinson and Caroline Catz

programme.title: Davina McCall: Life at the Extreme
programme.desc: Davina travels to the Costa Rican jungle, which is one of the wettest and most humid places on earth, but is a habitat in which animals thrive and is home to around 500,000 species. She learns about the ways in which creatures including sloths, poison dart frogs, tarantulas, tapirs, fer-de-lance vipers, common basilisks and spider monkeys have adapted to the conditions, before spending a night high up in the treetops of the rainforest - where a lightning striking directly overhead forces an abrupt change of plans

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot
programme.desc: The Belgian detective is summoned to a hotel by a mysterious veiled lady who tells him that her forthcoming marriage is under threat because she is being blackmailed over a love letter. Drama, starring David Suchet and Frances Barber

programme.title: Bless This House
programme.desc: Sid is not feeling his usual self, so he goes to see the doctor
